input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Coolant`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Coolant` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.1.6.3
            TITLE: the design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.3: The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples
T.1.6.3 The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples 
include but are not limited to:  exhaust pipe, coolant hose/tube, Accumulator Container) and 
the panel that the driver could contact (seat or floor): 
a. Conduction Isolation by one of the following: 
• No direct contact between the heat source and the panel 
• A heat resistant, conduction isolation material with a minimum thickness of 8 mm 
between the heat source and the panel  
b. Convection Isolation by a minimum air gap of 25 mm between the heat source and the 
panel 
c. Radiation Isolation by one of the following: 
• A solid metal heat shield with a minimum thickness of 0.4 mm 
• Reflective foil or tape when combined with conduction insulation

RULE: t.1.6.1
            TITLE: when seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.1: When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to
T.1.6.1 When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to 
ensure that the driver will not contact any metal or other materials which may become heated 
to a surface temperature above 60°C.

RULE: t.5.4.2
            TITLE: liquid coolant for electric motors, accumulators or hv electronics must be one of:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.4.2: Liquid coolant for electric motors, Accumulators or HV electronics must be one of:
T.5.4.2 Liquid coolant for electric motors, Accumulators or HV electronics must be one of: 
• plain water with no additives  
• oil

RULE: t.5.6.3
            TITLE: catch cans for the engine coolant system and engine lubrication system must have a minimum

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.6.3: Catch cans for the engine coolant system and engine lubrication system must have a minimum
T.5.6.3 Catch cans for the engine coolant system and engine lubrication system must have a minimum 
capacity of 10% of the fluid being contained or 0.9 liter, whichever is higher

RULE: t.5.4.3
            TITLE: (ev only) liquid coolant must not directly touch the cells in the accumulator

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.4.3: (EV only) Liquid coolant must not directly touch the cells in the Accumulator
T.5.4.3 (EV only) Liquid coolant must not directly touch the cells in the Accumulator

RULE: ic.2.5.1
            TITLE: the intake air may be cooled with an intercooler (a charge air cooler).

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.2.5.1: The intake air may be cooled with an intercooler (a charge air cooler).
IC.2.5.1 The intake air may be cooled with an intercooler (a charge air cooler).  
a. It must be located downstream of the throttle body  
b. Only ambient air may be used to remove heat from the intercooler system  
c. Air to air and water to air intercoolers are permitted  
d. The coolant of a water to air intercooler system must meet T.5.4.1

RULE: t.5.6.4
            TITLE: catch cans for any vent on other systems containing liquid lubricant or coolant, including a

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.6.4: Catch cans for any vent on other systems containing liquid lubricant or coolant, including a
T.5.6.4 Catch cans for any vent on other systems containing liquid lubricant or coolant, including a 
differential, gearbox, or electric motor, must have a minimum capacity of 10% of the fluid 
being contained or 0.5 liter, whichever is higher

RULE: f.11.2.3
            TITLE: all non crushable items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.3: All Non Crushable Items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the
F.11.2.3 All Non Crushable Items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the 
Accumulator Container. 
Non Crushable Items include, but are not limited to motors, differentials, and the side or rear 
impact structure itself.  Accumulator mounts do not require clearance

RULE: t.1.7.1
            TITLE: all vehicles must have a floor closeout to prevent track debris from entering

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.1: All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering
T.1.7.1 All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering

RULE: ic.7.2.3
            TITLE: any exhaust components (headers, mufflers, etc.) that protrude from the side of the body in

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.7.2.3: Any exhaust components (headers, mufflers, etc.) that protrude from the side of the body in
IC.7.2.3 Any exhaust components (headers, mufflers, etc.) that protrude from the side of the body in 
front of the Main Hoop must be shielded to prevent contact by persons approaching the 
vehicle or a driver exiting the vehicle

RULE: t.1.8.3
            TITLE: positioning

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.3: Positioning
T.1.8.3 Positioning  
The Firewall must extend sufficiently far upwards and/or rearwards and/or sideways where 
any point on the drivers body less than 100 mm above the bottom of the helmet of the tallest 
driver must not be in direct line of sight with any part given in T.1.8.1 above

RULE: t.2.1
            TITLE: harness definitions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.1: Harness Definitions
T.2.1 Harness Definitions 
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine 
Belt.  
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.  
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.   
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder 
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

RULE: t.1.8.4
            TITLE: details

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.4: Details
T.1.8.4 Details 
a. Firewalls composed of multiple panels must overlap and be sealed at the joints 
Sealing between firewalls must not be a stressed part of the Firewall 
b. Grommets must be used to seal any pass through for wiring, cables, etc
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 58 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Any seals or adhesives used with the Firewall must be rated for the application 
environment

RULE: f.1.9
            TITLE: impact attenuator

            CONTENT:
            rule F.1.9: Impact Attenuator
F.1.9 Impact Attenuator 
A deformable, energy absorbing device located forward of the Front Bulkhead.

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
F.11.2.2 Rear Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from rear impact:  
• When the rear impact structure is 100 mm or less from an Accumulator Container, 
the structure must be Equivalent to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
• When the rear impact structure is more than 100 mm from an Accumulator 
Container, the structure must meet  F.5.16 Component Protection 
• The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. The Rear Impact Protection for the Accumulator Container must: 
• Go to the Upper Side Impact Height at minimum
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 53 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the top of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Upper Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the bottom of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Lower Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: t.1.4.4
            TITLE: all vehicle controls must stay below the top-most point of the front hoop in any operational

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.4: All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational
T.1.4.4 All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational 
position

RULE: t.1.4.2
            TITLE: any mechanism in the throttle system that could become jammed must be covered.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.2: Any mechanism in the throttle system that could become jammed must be covered.
T.1.4.2 Any mechanism in the throttle system that could become jammed must be covered. 
This is to prevent debris or interference and includes but is not limited to a gear mechanism

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection 
d. Differential mounts or a plate behind upper and lower rear impact tubes may be used as 
Rear Impact Protection.   
If used, the mounts or plate must be 25 mm or more larger at both the top and bottom 
of the upper and lower tubes

RULE: f.11.2.1
            TITLE: side impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.1: Side Impact Protection
F.11.2.1 Side Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from side impact by structure Equivalent 
to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. Accumulator Container side impact protection must go to a minimum height that is the 
lower of the two:  
• The height of the Upper Side Impact Structure 
• The top of the Accumulator Container at that point 
c. Tractive System components other than Accumulator Containers in a position below 350 
mm from the ground must be protected from side impact by structure that meets F.5.16 
Component Protection

RULE: t.1.4.3
            TITLE: all vehicle controls (steering, gear change, cockpit main switch / cockpit shutdown button)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.3: All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button)
T.1.4.3 All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button) 
must be operated from inside the cockpit without any part of the driver, including hands, arms 
or elbows, being outside of: 
a. The Side Impact Structure defined in F.6.4 / F.7.5 
b. Two longitudinal vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the 
uppermost member of the Side Impact Structure
